status: draft — CPA sign-off required before production

notes.purpose: Machine-readable IRS category taxonomy. Consumed by Intelligence Service (categorization), Export Service (Schedule C / 1120-S / 1065 PDF), Tax Service (quarterly estimates, QBI), and AI Eval 04 (Financial Computation).

entityPaths.smllc: Schedule C (Form 1040) + Schedule SE — disregarded entity, same lines as sole prop
entityPaths.mmllc: Form 1065 + Schedule K-1 → members' Schedule E Part II; K-1 Box 14 carries SE earnings for active members
entityPaths.sCorp: Form 1120-S + Schedule K-1 → shareholder's Schedule E Part II; shareholder's W-2 wages subject to FICA

criticalFixes_v1_0: Form 1065 Line 18 is Retirement plans — NOT insurance (v1.1 had this wrong)
Form 1065 Line 11 is Repairs and maintenance — dedicated line, NOT Line 20
Form 1065 Line 10 is Guaranteed payments to partners — NOT external commissions/contractors
Form 1065 Line 9 is W-2 employee wages — NOT guaranteed payments
S-Corp K-1 distributions go on Box 16 Code D — NOT Box 19 (Box 19 is partnership K-1)
2025 standard mileage rate is 70¢/mile per IRS Notice 2025-5 (was 67¢ in 2024)
De minimis safe harbor is $2,500/item for non-AFS taxpayers per Treas. Reg. §1.263(a)-1(f) — NOT $500
MMLLC active members pay SE tax on K-1 Box 1 ordinary income (Renkemeyer 136 T.C. 137) — materially different from S-Corp treatment
Reasonable compensation for S-Corp has no statutory % threshold — use IRS Fact Sheet 2008-25 nine factors

incomeLines.line4: Cost of goods sold (from Line 42 in Part III)

expenseLines.line20a: Rent or lease — vehicles, machinery, equipment
expenseLines.line20b: Rent or lease — other business property
expenseLines.line30: Expenses for business use of home (Form 8829 or simplified)

partIIILines.line33: Method used to value closing inventory

k1Boxes.box1: Ordinary business income (loss) → flows to shareholder's Schedule E Part II; NOT subject to SE tax
k1Boxes.box16: Items affecting shareholder basis — Code D = Distributions
k1Boxes.note: Form 1120-S Schedule K-1 ends at Box 17 — Box 19 does NOT exist on S-Corp K-1

deductionLines.line9: Salaries and wages (other than to partners) less employment credits
deductionLines.line10: Guaranteed payments to partners (subject to SE tax via K-1 Box 4a)
deductionLines.line16b: Less depreciation reported on Form 1125-A or elsewhere
deductionLines.line18: Retirement plans, etc. (NOT insurance — critical v1.1 error corrected)
deductionLines.line19: Employee benefit programs (health, fringe for non-partners)

k1Lines.box1: Ordinary business income (loss) → flows to partner's Schedule E Part II
k1Lines.box14: Self-employment earnings (A/B/C codes) → Schedule SE for active members per Renkemeyer 136 T.C. 137
k1Lines.line19: Distributions — Code A (cash), B (marketable securities), C (other property)

2026.standardMileageRate: TBD — IRS publishes in December 2025 Notice
2026.qbiLowerThreshold: TBD — IRS publishes Rev. Proc. late 2025

criticalSunsets.tcja_274o_employerMeals: TCJA §13304(a)(2) scheduled to reduce employer-provided meals on premises from 50% to 0% deductible in 2026. Verify OBBBA did not repeal this sunset.
criticalSunsets.tcja_miscDeductions: TCJA §11045 suspended 2% miscellaneous itemized deductions through 2025. Verify whether OBBBA extended or let them return.

categories.label: Client income
categories.notes: Gross receipts. Cash basis: record when received. For PCI/HIPAA: store payer name only, never end-client PHI.

categories.label: Sales income
categories.notes: Record GROSS sales. Platform fees (Stripe, Shopify, Etsy) are a separate expense, NOT netted from revenue.

categories.label: Subscription income
categories.notes: Stripe MRR: record gross payouts, book Stripe fees separately as expense.

categories.label: Insurance income
categories.notes: Healthcare insurance reimbursements. HIPAA: use payer (insurer) name only, never patient name. Recognize when ERA/remittance received.

categories.label: Tip income
categories.notes: Cash/Venmo tips are taxable income per IRC §61. Must be reported even if cash-only.

categories.label: Job payment
categories.notes: Trades: payment on job completion.

categories.label: Product income
categories.notes: If physical product with cost → corresponding COGS via Part III / Form 1125-A.

categories.label: Cost of goods
categories.recognitionRule: Recognized when goods are SOLD, not when purchased. Inventory is an asset until sold (§471).
categories.notes: Retail: finished goods purchased for resale. Product businesses must track opening and closing inventory.

categories.label: Inventory (COGS)
categories.notes: Inventory = asset until sold. §471(c) small-taxpayer method available for taxpayers under $30M gross receipts (2025 threshold).

categories.label: Food & ingredients (COGS)
categories.notes: Raw ingredients consumed in food prep → Line 38 (Materials and supplies). Finished goods bought for resale → Line 36.

categories.label: Packaging & supplies (COGS)
categories.notes: Packaging that becomes part of the sold product → COGS. Cleaning supplies / non-product supplies → Line 22 / Line 20.

categories.label: Advertising
categories.notes: Form 1120-S has a dedicated Advertising line (16); Form 1065 routes to Line 20 Other deductions (no dedicated line).

categories.label: Marketing
categories.notes: Same as Advertising. Penny should consolidate to one canonical label.

categories.label: Vehicle fuel
categories.notes: Under standard mileage method, fuel is NOT separately deductible (absorbed in per-mile rate). Under actual method, fuel is a component. 2025 rate: 70¢/mi (Notice 2025-5). Q-C3 method election must be resolved at vehicle onboarding.

categories.label: Van lease
categories.notes: Lease payments are Line 20a (Sch C) / Line 11 (1120-S) / Line 13 (1065). §280F luxury auto inclusion amount may apply if FMV exceeds threshold ($60,300 for 2025 passenger autos). NEVER combine with fuel into a single label — fuel goes to a different line.

categories.label: Vehicle depreciation & loan interest
categories.notes: Compound category. Loan payment must be split into principal (non-deductible), interest (Line 16b etc.), and depreciation (computed on schedule, not tied to cash outflow). If standard mileage elected, depreciation is absorbed by the per-mile rate and only interest is separately deductible.

categories.label: Platform fees
categories.notes: Shopify, Etsy, Amazon, eBay, Square, Stripe marketplace fees. Form 1065 Line 10 is NOT for these — that's Guaranteed payments to partners. External commissions go to Line 20 on 1065.

categories.label: Contractors
categories.notes: External 1099 contractors. Form 1065: Line 20 (NOT Line 9 — Line 9 is W-2 employee wages; NOT Line 10 — Line 10 is partner guaranteed payments). 1099-NEC required if individual paid ≥$600/yr AND payment not made via credit card / third-party settlement (those get 1099-K from processor). W-9 required before first payment to avoid backup withholding §3406.

categories.label: Subcontractors
categories.notes: Same as contractors — 1099-NEC rules apply if individual/unincorporated and ≥$600/yr.

categories.label: Payroll (W-2 employees)
categories.notes: W-2 wages to non-owner employees. Employer share of FICA (7.65%) → Line 23 / Line 12 / Line 14. Form 941 quarterly, Form 940 annually, state UI separate.

categories.label: Shareholder payroll / Officer compensation
categories.notes: S-Corp only. IRC §1366 requires reasonable compensation to shareholder-employees (IRS Fact Sheet 2008-25 nine factors). Case law: Watson v. U.S., Glass Blocks Unlimited. No statutory % threshold. Defense is a compensation study (RCReports/BLS comparables), NOT a % ratio.

categories.label: Guaranteed payments to partners
categories.notes: MMLLC only (Form 1065). Treated as payment for services regardless of partnership income. Subject to SE tax via K-1 Box 4a. NOT to be confused with distributions (Line 19).

categories.label: Commercial insurance
categories.notes: General liability, commercial property, E&O, workers' comp. Form 1065 Line 20 (NOT Line 18 — Line 18 is Retirement plans). Form 1120-S Line 19 Other deductions (insurance for NON-OWNER employees → Line 18 Employee benefit programs).

categories.label: Professional liability insurance
categories.notes: E&O, malpractice. Same routing as commercial insurance.

categories.label: Health insurance (W-2 employees)
categories.notes: For W-2 employees ONLY. NOT for >2% S-Corp shareholder — see owners-health-insurance-scorp.

categories.label: Owner's health insurance (sole prop)
categories.notes: Self-employed health insurance deduction. §162(l). NOT on Schedule C. Limited to earned income from the business.

categories.label: Owner's health insurance (S-Corp ≥2% shareholder)
categories.notes: IRC §1372(a) + §162(l) + Notice 2008-1. Premiums added to W-2 Box 1 wages (taxable income tax, NOT FICA). S-Corp takes compensation deduction on Line 7 (officer) via payroll, NOT on Line 18. Shareholder then deducts on personal Schedule 1 Line 17, capped at shareholder's W-2 wages from S-Corp.

categories.note: Marker category for Penny to split Gusto payroll entries per D72

categories.label: Accounting
categories.notes: CPA fees, bookkeeping, tax prep. Schedule C Line 17 (Legal and professional services) — NOT Line 27a.

categories.label: Legal fees
categories.notes: Business legal fees only. Attorney payments ALWAYS trigger 1099-MISC Box 10 (attorney exception to the corporate 1099 exemption).

categories.label: Office supplies
categories.notes: Administrative consumables: paper, ink, small office items. For business-production materials, use 'Supplies' (Line 22 on Sch C).

categories.label: Supplies
categories.notes: Materials used in business production (not for resale). De minimis safe harbor items <$2,500 land here.

categories.label: Tools & equipment

ifOverDeMinimis.decision: Section 179 vs. bonus depreciation vs. MACRS depreciation
ifOverDeMinimis.form1065Note: Form 4562 attached; Line 16c is the net deduction (16a gross − 16b reported on 1125-A or elsewhere)

categories.notes: De minimis safe harbor $2,500/item per Treas. Reg. §1.263(a)-1(f)(1)(ii) for non-AFS taxpayers. Annual election statement required.

categories.label: Hardware
categories.notes: Mixed personal/business use → business % only. Penny asks once.

categories.label: Office sublease
categories.notes: If subleasing from an individual landlord: 1099-MISC Box 1 required for rent ≥$600/yr. Corporate landlords exempt.

categories.label: Equipment rental
categories.notes: Vehicles, machinery, equipment. Sch C has separate 20a (equipment) / 20b (other property); 1120-S / 1065 combine to one Rents line.

categories.label: Home office
categories.sCorpNote: S-Corp shareholder-employee cannot take home office on personal return (misc. itemized deduction suspended 2018-2025 by TCJA §11045). Use accountable plan under §1.62-2 to reimburse; S-Corp deducts on Line 19.
testsRequired: principal place of business OR regular client meetings

categories.label: Repairs and maintenance
categories.notes: Form 1065: Line 11 (NOT Line 20 — Line 11 is dedicated to Repairs). BAR test under Treas. Reg. §1.263(a)-3: Betterment/Adaptation/Restoration = capitalize; maintenance = expense. Routine maintenance safe harbor §1.263(a)-3(i) available.

categories.label: License renewal & permits
categories.notes: Business licenses, contractor licenses, health permits.

categories.label: Professional memberships
categories.notes: Trade associations (NECA, NASW, state bars for business). NOT Line 23 — these are not taxes/licenses.

categories.label: Payroll taxes (employer share)
categories.notes: Employer share of FICA (6.2% SS + 1.45% Medicare), FUTA, SUI. Trust fund liability under §6672 if unpaid.

categories.label: Travel
categories.notes: Overnight trips away from tax home only. Local client trips go to Vehicle (Line 9). §274(d) substantiation: date, purpose, destination, amount.

categories.label: Business meals (50%)
categories.notes: §274(n)(1) 50% limit. §274(d) substantiation: who, purpose, location, date, amount. Receipt required if ≥$75/person. Penny must split: record 100% of cost, show 50% as deductible.
exceptions_100Pct: Meals sold as inventory (restaurant own menu items) — §274(e)(8)
exceptions_0Pct: Entertainment portion of meal + entertainment bill — §274(a)(1)(A); Notice 2018-76
Employer-provided meals on premises for convenience of employer — §274(n)(2)(B) drops to 0% starting 2026 per TCJA §13304 sunset (verify OBBBA impact)

categories.label: Client meals (50%)
categories.notes: Same rule as business meals. Client presence does not change 50% rule.

categories.label: Entertainment (not deductible)
categories.notes: Post-TCJA, entertainment is 0% deductible per §274(a)(1)(A). If a bill combines meal + entertainment (e.g., dinner at a game), the meal portion may be 50% deductible if separately stated and documented. Notice 2018-76.

categories.label: Utilities
categories.notes: Dedicated business location: Line 25 at 100%. Home-based: utilities flow through Form 8829 at business-use %.

categories.label: Phone
categories.notes: Dedicated business line → 100%. Shared personal/business phone → business-use % only. §274(d)(4) removed cell phones from listed-property status (Small Business Jobs Act §2043, 2010), so §162 substantiation applies.
categories.askOnce: Is this a dedicated business phone or shared personal/business?

categories.label: Internet
categories.notes: Home-based: routes through Form 8829. Dedicated office: Line 25 direct at 100%.

categories.label: Solo 401(k) contribution (employer portion)
categories.notes: Employer portion (25% of compensation). Employee deferral portion goes on personal Schedule 1 Line 16, NOT on the entity-level return.

categories.label: SEP-IRA contribution
categories.notes: For sole props: deducted on personal Schedule 1 Line 16, NOT on Schedule C. For S-Corps: employer portion on 1120-S Line 17.

categories.label: Software subscriptions
categories.notes: Notion, Adobe CC, GitHub, Zoom, etc. Sch C: Part V itemized → Line 27a. Canonical label — consolidates prior variants Software / Software & tools / Software & SaaS tools.

categories.label: Cloud infrastructure
categories.notes: AWS, GCP, Vercel, Cloudflare.

categories.label: Professional development
categories.notes: Workshops, certifications, conferences directly related to current business. NOT for preparation for a new career (not deductible).

categories.label: Continuing education
categories.notes: Must maintain or improve skills in CURRENT business. Healthcare CE credits fit this; switching career fields does not.

categories.label: Miscellaneous business expenses
categories.notes: Catch-all with DESCRIPTION REQUIRED. Penny must prompt user for a specific description (e.g., 'Bank wire fee', 'Trade association dues') — a bare 'Miscellaneous' entry is an audit trigger. Each item flows to Schedule C Part V as a named line item.

categories.label: Owner's distribution (S-Corp)
categories.notes: IRC §1368. Reduces AAA and stock basis. Distributions in excess of basis are capital gain (§1368(b)(2)). Form 7203 basis tracking required 2021+.

categories.label: Member distribution (MMLLC)

lineMap.k1: Line 19 Code A (cash) / B (marketable securities) / C (other)

categories.notes: IRC §731. Reduces basis. Excess over basis is capital gain.

categories.label: Owner's draw (sole prop / SMLLC)
categories.notes: Not a deduction. Drawing account tracks owner's equity withdrawals. Income is taxed at the Schedule C net profit level regardless of how much the owner draws.

postCpaReview_openItems.issue: Vehicle method election (standard mileage vs. actual)
postCpaReview_openItems.status: partially resolved by stress test — Penny default is standard mileage with per-mile rate from taxYearConstants; needs CPA sign-off on switching rules precision per Rev. Proc. 2019-46
needs product stance — recommend simplified default, surface actual when annual savings > $500; add §1250 recapture warning and S-Corp accountable plan path
OPEN — GC review required. Determines liability surface and required disclosures.
OPEN — verify effective dates, §174 R&E retroactivity, §274(o) meal sunset status, 1099-K final threshold against current IRS publications
PROPOSED — classification table in taxonomy doc Part 2; needs CPA validation for borderline cases (P15 software developer, P19 VA)
OPEN — integrate RCReports, BLS OES, or Payscale API; abandon % heuristic

cpaSignOffRequired: Every lineMap cell for every category
All SSTB classifications in QBI section
§471(c) small-taxpayer inventory election for trades personas
De minimis safe harbor default election
